result:
Done. Full email/password auth is in place and verified end-to-end against the live server — signup, duplicate-email rejection, login, `/auth/me`, logout, wrong-password rejection, password reset (including token single-use and old-session revocation), and the login rate limiter returning 429 after 10 attempts all behaved correctly, and `tsc --noEmit` passes.

What was added (new dependencies: `pg`, `zod`, `nodemailer`, `cookie-parser`):

- **`src/db.ts`** — pg pool plus idempotent startup migration creating `users`, `sessions`, and `password_reset_tokens` tables.
- **`src/password.ts`** — scrypt hashing via Node's built-in crypto (OWASP parameters, per-user salt, `timingSafeEqual` comparison, self-describing hash format so parameters can be upgraded later).
- **`src/session.ts`** — server-side sessions: a random 256-bit token in an `httpOnly`/`sameSite=lax` cookie (`secure` in production), stored SHA-256-hashed in Postgres with a 30-day expiry; `loadSession` middleware and `requireAuth` guard.
- **`src/validation.ts`** — zod schemas (email normalized to lowercase, password 8–128 chars, strict bodies) with structured field-level error responses.
- **`src/rateLimit.ts`** — fixed-window in-memory limiter keyed by IP: 10 login attempts per 15 minutes, plus 5 reset requests per hour. It's single-process; the comment notes to swap the store for Redis/Postgres if you scale horizontally.
- **`src/mail.ts`** — nodemailer using SMTP env vars; with no SMTP configured it logs the reset link to the console (dev fallback).
- **`src/routes/auth.ts`** — the six endpoints (listed in the README). Security details: login verifies against a dummy hash for unknown emails so timing doesn't leak account existence; reset requests always return the same generic response; reset tokens are stored hashed, expire in 1 hour, are consumed atomically (no reuse), and a successful reset revokes all of the user's sessions.
- **`src/index.ts`** — wiring plus hardening: 64 kb JSON body limit, `x-powered-by` disabled, JSON error handler that doesn't leak internals, `trust proxy` in production so rate limiting sees real client IPs, and graceful shutdown.

The README documents the endpoints and the environment variables (`APP_BASE_URL`, SMTP settings, etc.). Nothing is committed yet — say the word if you'd like a commit.
